title_en: How to Spend the Advertising Budget
excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "How to Spend the Advertising Budget." ▼Masu alone won't spread the word. Now, I'd like to write about FOMUS promotions. FOMUS started in 2
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about

"How to Spend the Advertising Budget."

▼Masu Alone Won't Spread the Word

Now, I'd like to write about FOMUS promotions.

FOMUS started in February 2021,

and three years have passed.

Within six months of launching,

I realized that sustaining a business

by selling only masu was

an extremely high hurdle,

and right around that time, the AI and NFT boom arrived,

so I decided to ride that wave.

First-mover advantage really is important,

and I used the revenue from AI and NFT businesses

to build the budget for the FOMUS brand.

*Plus income from photography work

When I write it out like this,

you might wonder if there's even a point in continuing FOMUS,

but

FOMUS brings me things that

money can never buy.

Things like connections between people, and memories.

From those connections,

new projects have started.

FOMUS is a communication tool

that connects people,

and that function is very much alive and active.

However,

there are both advantages and disadvantages to this.

The advantage is that people other than me who own a FOMUS masu

can bring it along when they go out for drinks and people find it interesting.

On top of that, it can lead to becoming friends.

Also, the bonds between people who own the same masu deepen.

There are other advantages too,

but today I want to focus on the disadvantages.

That is, unless I'm constantly active,

it's difficult for FOMUS to spread.

As someone whose mission is to spread the FOMUS brand worldwide,

I need to make a bigger impact.

However, due to my own lack of ability,

a brand doesn't grow that easily

or spread.

Unless I improve my own skills,

the brand won't grow beyond that.

That's why, right now,

I'm focused on activities like raising funds and gathering people.

And I had also prepared an

"advertising budget" to use while I'm here in the Philippines.

That's because, during my study abroad in Cebu,

I needed to attract people for "Masu Photo" shoots -- taking photos with masu on my days off.

As I've written many times in this activity log,

I've been running ads on Instagram to attract people.

In fact, in Cebu,

over 100 people have made offers.

This is possible because of the concept of taking photos with "masu," a traditional Japanese craft, and

if it were just "taking nice photos,"

I wouldn't receive this many offers.

And then, once we've made plans for a Masu Photo shoot

and we talk on the day of the shoot,

that time is incredibly important.

Because that's when I convey the fun of FOMUS.

This is where my English skills are put to the test, haha.

But there are real skills I've gained from studying abroad,

and explaining FOMUS has become overwhelmingly easier than before.

And models who resonate with the concept

voluntarily promote FOMUS on their own.

This way, I can get FOMUS known

to audiences I can't reach myself.

This approach involves investing advertising money into Instagram,

finding local models,

and having them promote FOMUS.

And now,

I'm going to unlock something here in Cebu

that I've never even done in Japan.

That is, paying models

PR fees to promote FOMUS.

Until now, due to budget constraints

and wanting to build a community around FOMUS,

I hadn't paid anyone to take photos.

However, as I mentioned earlier,

neither I nor FOMUS currently have strong influence.

Moreover, FOMUS has launched a masu accessory brand called "FOMUS Parure,"

and I'm being very selective about models for that.

The goal is to reach the level of high-end brands like Tiffany and Van Cleef, and I feel we need professional models for this.

Masu Photo works best when shot with local models, while for the accessory promotions, we need serious professional models to wear them.

Furthermore, the professional models will also cooperate with Masu Photo shoots,

so the anyone-can-join Masu Photo remains intact,

without damaging the image of the high-end accessory brand "Parure."

So,

this April I'm setting up appointments with professional models

and moving things forward.

The shoots will be happening very soon, so

when that time comes,

I'd be happy if you'd think, "Ah, so that's the strategy MaSU is using,

that's why he's working with pros."

That said, whether this clicks or not

won't be clear until we actually try it,

and honestly, the money is flying out faster than expected.

I had prepared an advertising budget,

but I'm going to use almost all of it up.

If this promotion leads to sales

of FOMUS masu and accessories,

then spending the advertising budget will have been the right call,

and if they don't sell, I'll know I need to adjust the approach.

I feel like I need to keep exploring various approaches in this area.

To spread FOMUS,

it looks like in the fourth year, I'll be doing Masu Photo shoots with serious professional models.

I wrote about how I'm spending the advertising budget

this time around.

Alright then, I'll keep pushing forward with preparations.

excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Continuing is Important." ▼Building Habits. Now, it's been 60 days since I came to Cebu. As I've written many times, time really flies.
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about

"Continuing is Important."

▼Building Habits

Now, it's been 60 days since I came to Cebu.

As I've written many times, time really flies.

A year has 365 days (366 this year),

and I've already spent 60 of them

in the Philippines.

They say each year feels shorter as you get older,

and it's faster than I expected.

With my activities in Cebu,

there are plenty of things you can turn into habits over 60 days.

Here's what I've managed to make into habits during this period...

(1) Waking up at 6 AM

(2) Memorizing 20 English words daily

(3) Running 5-8 km, 6 days a week

(4) Writing a 1,500-3,000 character Cebu activity log every day

There are other smaller things too,

but I'll leave it at these for now.

First, "waking up at 6 AM."

This is because there's a special class (vocabulary test) at 7 AM on weekdays, and I need to study for it.

After getting ready, from 6:30 I study alone

on the rooftop for 30 minutes.

This special class is

available only for those who want to take it,

and about 10 people are enrolled.

In this special class,

there's about 45 minutes of vocabulary study starting at 7 PM.

In my case,

I partly signed up for the class

to build the habit.

Moreover, this class is taught by

one of the best teachers at the school,

so even though it's vocabulary, the lessons are fun.

So anyway,

I've successfully made memorizing vocabulary a daily habit.

And the third one --

running 5-8 km, 6 times a week.

As I wrote in a previous entry,

I've committed to running the Medoc Marathon held in Pauillac, France this September.

It's an absolutely crazy event where you have to run about 42 km

in costume while drinking wine.

Normally, I'd never sign up for something like this, but

I figured that to spread the FOMUS brand worldwide,

I should participate in events where people gather from all over the world.

Plus, the idea of my first-ever marathon being the Medoc Marathon sounded pretty cool, so I roped in some friends and we're making it happen.

I definitely want the bragging rights of having completed 42 km,

so I've been running since now to prepare.

Here's what today's run looked like.

Well well,

I've built the habit,

and on top of that, for someone who never used to run at all,

starting at this point,

compared to when I first began,

I can see I've improved tremendously.

It really drives home the importance of consistency.

And the last one, number four --

writing a 1,500-3,000 character Cebu activity log every day.

In other words, this very article!

I've been writing this since two days before coming to Cebu,

and today makes entry number 62.

Somehow, I've been keeping at it diligently.

This is meaningful because I'm organizing my thoughts

while sharing what I've learned.

The emotions and feelings I have right now

can only be captured at this moment.

I still have a diary from elementary school at my parents' house,

but I can't write that diary now.

That's exactly why memories and places

are incredibly important.

In the future, when I become more well-known,

having these memories recorded from this time

is really powerful.

It's great to be able to leave behind articles that can only be written right now.

*It's also easy to repurpose for business

And so,

all sorts of things have become habits.

I was also sending daily emails to models initially, but since the April shoots are going to cost a ton of money,

I've paused any further offers to professional models here in Cebu.

Still, the fact that I'm able to

live such a fulfilling life

is because I'm blessed with a great environment around me,

and I'm incredibly grateful.

But every day, when the day is ending

and I'm writing this article,

I often pass out.

I need to build up more stamina!!

excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "The Fatigue Had Piled Up." ▼Sorry! I was a day late writing this article. Lately, I've been running for an hour every day, and on top of that,
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about

"The Fatigue Had Piled Up."

▼Sorry!

I was a day late writing this article.

Lately, I've been running for an hour every day,

and on top of that, I've been thankfully busy with work,

and while I was buried in tasks,

my body wasn't recovering fast enough,

and before I knew it, I was heading to

a massage place near the school without thinking.

It was a foot-only massage,

and I had planned to write the article during that time,

but it felt so good that I fell asleep.

Also, after getting back,

I was handling the paperwork to start

recruiting models for my activities in Manila after graduation.

I'm running ads for this one too.

So, what kind of results will we get??

I think we'll find out starting tomorrow.

▼AI Illustrations Are Leveling Up Too

Now, one of my jobs is

the "KUKU x Music" project

that I post every Saturday.

I'm in charge of creating the illustration backgrounds

and posting them,

and while creating illustrations themselves is easy,

they need to match the vibe of the music,

so you need skills beyond just being able to make AI illustrations.

However, I've been creating images for over 2 years

and editing images for about 6 years,

so I can understand what kind of background would suit each track

and what prompts to use to get the background I'm aiming for.

That's why I can create accurate backgrounds in a short time.

https://www.notion.so

The great thing about this kind of work is that

I can do it from anywhere, whether in Japan or overseas.

So anyway,

this entry is a bit short, but to make up for it, the next post will be packed with content!

See you next time!

excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Preparing for the Manila Battle." ▼Use what you can. Now, the Cebu stay is almost over! I'll be active in Cebu until April 26, and from the 27th to the 30th
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about

"Preparing for the Manila Battle."

▼Use What You Can

Now, the Cebu stay is almost over!

I'll be active in Cebu until April 26,

and from the 27th to the 30th, I'll be based in Manila/Makati.

I've already booked the flights and hotel,

and basically, I plan to do Masu Photo shoots

to spread the FOMUS brand.

If there's free time, I'll do a bit of sightseeing.

So, I've started running Instagram ads again, which already proved effective in Cebu.

Additionally, as an experiment this time, I've also run ads on Facebook in addition to Instagram.

It costs twice as much,

but I'm running both an Instagram Reel ad

and a Facebook post ad simultaneously

to see which one generates more offers.

As of now,

about 20 hours since launching the ads,

the Facebook ad seems to be driving slightly more traffic.

Honestly, even if you run ads, if the content or service isn't interesting, nobody will send you a message.

However, as of now, about 30 messages have come in.

To be honest, I haven't been able to check them all yet,

but I'm predicting around 70 messages will come in over this weekend.

This all depends on the balance with ad spending,

but I think it's a good way to get more people to know about FOMUS on a low budget.

What boosts these ads further

is the actual Masu Photo shoots themselves.

So, what will the results look like?

I'll report back when the results are in.

▼With My Roommates

Now, from here on it's just a diary entry.

After school classes ended,

I went out for Korean BBQ (samgyeopsal) with my roommates.

My roommates are a Chinese guy, a Korean guy, and me -- three of us.

We chat a lot in the room too, so the atmosphere is really good.

Of course, we all speak different languages,

so everything is in English.

Being able to create an environment where you speak English outside of class is huge.

After the Korean BBQ,

we headed to our regular bar near the school.

Some teachers were already drinking there, so we joined them.

After that, the three of us went to a massage place near the school.

We got a solid hour of refreshing relaxation.

Basically, all I do in Cebu is study English or work on business, but time like this is actually important too.

Because I understand that these are precious moments and experiences unique to studying abroad, I think carefully about how to spend each day.

The fact that I can make these kinds of decisions is because I've gained various experiences as a working adult, and all in all, I'm just having a great time right now.

I'll continue enjoying every day while spreading the FOMUS brand even further!

excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Photo Shoot with Professional Models!" ▼Walking the talk. Now, I arrived in Cebu in early February. Two months have passed since then. On school holidays
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about

"Photo Shoot with Professional Models!"

▼Walking the Talk

Now, I arrived in Cebu in early February.

Two months have passed since then.

On school holidays, I've been working as a photographer to spread the FOMUS brand,

and simultaneously, I was also sending out

tons of emails to professional models.

I'm very grateful that

in February, many local subjects

participated,

and I've already shot enough people to make a "Masu Photo Book in the Philippines,"

but since I was here, I wanted to work with pros too, so I was approaching them behind the scenes.

And then, on April 6, 2024,

I finally had my long-awaited photo shoot with professional models.

Six people gathered in total.

Some were models who responded to my emails,

and one of my class teachers

introduced me to a model friend of hers.

The venue was not Cebu Island but a resort hotel on Mactan Island, where the airport is.

It was Bluewater Maribago.

For the venue, I figured if I'm inviting pros, I need to do it right! So I booked a hotel with a private beach.

That said, we weren't staying overnight,

so we used the day-use option this time.

Pool, beach, and lunch included for 1,600 pesos per person.

About 4,300 yen.

Staying overnight would be super expensive,

but with day-use, you can spend the day relatively cheaply.

This time, there were 8 of us: the models, me, and the teacher who brought the models (she was my manager for the day).

Of course, we did FOMUS Masu Photo shoots,

and since this also included promotion for the masu accessory brand "FOMUS Parure," everyone wore the accessories.

Back in January, I had Yuri-san, who makes the accessories,

create extra samples, and now they're coming in super handy!!

*Just in case something like this came up

*Thinking ahead is important

I brought enough accessories for all 8 people, so during group shots, nobody was left without something to wear.

Then, one by one, Masu Photo shoots.

About 10-15 minutes per person.

*With 6 people, that alone takes over an hour.

Normally I'd chat with each person while shooting, but this time I had to be efficient.

Of course, we were still talking.

After the individual shoots,

we did a group shoot with everyone together.

I had asked the models to wear black outfits,

and when everyone was together, it looked incredibly cool!!

Seven people in black on the beach -- it made for quite an unusual shoot, haha.

And at this Bluewater Maribago,

there's a deserted island you can go to for free.

I chose this location because of the beautiful sea and my interest in this island, and it was the perfect call.

The shooting was so easy!!

Because the sea was beautiful,

and the models were having a great time!

What matters most in a shoot is the vibe,

so if the setting is great, it makes for a fun shoot.

On the deserted island, we did pair shoots in twos.

Until now, I'd mostly done individual shoots,

but I realized during the shoot that pair shots work great too.

And for the finale, one more group Masu Photo with everyone.

When all the models in black gathered together, the impact was amazing,

and we got truly wonderful photos.

At the very end, I jumped in for a photo too.

Basically, it wouldn't look right for me and the teacher to go out together as just a man and woman,

so she worked behind the scenes all day.

Thanks to her, she was able to show us how to get to the island and film behind-the-scenes footage of my shoots.

*Super grateful

We were supposed to meet at 9 and start shooting at 10,

but of course, nobody arrived on time,

so we ended up starting at 10:30.

But we finished all the shooting in about 1 hour and 30 minutes,

and from noon, we all had lunch together!

To be honest,

I kind of wondered if the models gathered

because the lunch was included, haha.

But yeah, a resort hotel lunch buffet

is really a blast!

I love it too, so I was totally satisfied!

During lunch, I was able to talk with the models about various things, getting them to know FOMUS better while also improving my English.

Practical English really is the best way to learn!!

After lunch, everyone was free to go!

Stay and relax at the resort,

or head home.

I went to a bar with the models who stayed.

We had lots of great conversations there too!

During the chat, they asked, "Wanna go to a club tonight?"

Clubs aren't really my thing,

but since it was a good opportunity, I agreed to go.

Then, at 5 PM, I headed home for a bit.

A random incident happened --

the taxi driver the hotel called for us

said midway, "My mother's condition has worsened, I need to go back," and dropped us off in some totally unfamiliar place, haha.

Luckily, there was another taxi nearby,

so I grabbed a new one and made it home.

After getting home, I took a shower and rested for a bit.

Never thought I'd be heading to a club after a photo shoot, haha.

▼To the Club

I went to the club with 2 of the models I'd gathered

and my Korean roommate.

When we arrived,

a male friend of one of the models was already there,

so we joined up with him.

It was a club so it was crazy loud,

but hey, it was a good experience of an overseas club.

From around 11 PM to 4 AM,

we danced and drank on repeat.

Then after the club, we went out for more drinks,

so we ended up going until 7 AM, haha.

Counting from the morning shoot,

I was going full throttle for 24 hours straight.

So yeah,

it was a day of shooting with professional models on a private beach

and then dancing and drinking at a club at night!

Only 20 days left of life in Cebu.

I'll make the most of it with no regrets!

excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Office Work Day." ▼Getting things in order. Before getting to the main topic, a quick side story. Dancing and drinking at a club until 4 AM, then drinks at a bar after
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about

"Office Work Day."

▼Getting Things in Order

Before getting to the main topic, a quick side story.

Since it was a day of dancing and drinking at a club until 4 AM,

then going to a bar for more drinks,

I didn't get home until around 8 AM.

At the school where I'm staying,

if you don't submit a travel form in advance,

the curfew on weekdays is 10 PM,

and on Fridays and Saturdays it's midnight.

Since going to the club was a same-day decision,

I hadn't submitted a travel form or anything,

so I basically broke curfew.

If you break curfew, you get a "warning,"

and if you accumulate 4 warnings, you get expelled.

In my case, I'm graduating in 20 more days,

and I've never gotten a warning before,

so I went to the club prepared to take the hit this time.

I'll probably get called in tomorrow morning, haha.

Alright, enough about that --

today was a full day of office work.

I steadily worked through my backlog of tasks

while also editing yesterday's Masu Photo shoot.

Because there were so many photos

and it was a group shoot with 6 people,

the editing takes a lot of time.

The FOMUS masu logo needs to be clearly visible, and I usually adjust each one during editing to make the logo stand out.

This time, there were 6 masu in a single photo,

so it simply took 6 times as long, haha.

In the end, the editing took about 3 hours.

It would be nice if this could be automated

and easily fixed with AI.

Of course, I can download the same edits from the previous photo, but each one still needs to be checked individually,

so it's not that simple.

On top of that, I'm also simultaneously building connections with overseas companies.

All of that communication is in English,

so I'm managing with the help of ChatGPT.

That took about 2 hours as well.

Beyond that, there's contacting models for the Manila recruitment,

replying to tons of emails, and so on.

There's plenty to do.

On top of all that, I need to handle FOMUS's finances too.

Starting this week, we're also launching a crowdfunding campaign, which makes things even busier.

*The site is already built

Projects are spread across many areas,

and on weekdays, I'm immersed in language study from 7 AM to 8 PM.

I have some spare time here and there to work,

but I don't have as much time as I do in Japan,

so I need to get each task done faster.

There's also the matter of adding new designs for the character KUKU, which I need to compile into documents and send to the designer, but I haven't been able to start on that yet.

It's not super urgent, so the priority ends up being low.

Still, the sooner the better, since it can be used in KUKU's music short movies. I want to push it forward.

In this area, I still haven't been able to systematize things,

and I need to do everything by myself.

To become even more efficient,

I need to secure budget, handle marketing, and more.

There's a lot that needs to be done.

I may have sounded like I was showing off about being busy,

but honestly, I should have left the club earlier and gone home, but I couldn't bring myself to leave, and ended up staying until morning.

Not being able to make rational decisions like that might be my weakness.

While all of this was going on,

the day is coming to an end.

It was a day of staying indoors

and steadily grinding through office work.

School starts again tomorrow.

However, in the Philippines,

Tuesday and Wednesday this week are holidays, so no school.

That means I can dedicate a lot of time to work.

That said, one day is already booked for a model shoot.

This shoot is by far the most expensive promotion I've done in Cebu.

I'll prepare thoroughly

and carry out the promotion!

excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "I Got a Warning." ▼No way... As I wrote in the last article, going to the club was decided on the same day as the Saturday shoot, and I actually went, but
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about

"I Got a Warning."

▼No Way...

As I wrote in the last article,

going to the club was decided on the same day as the Saturday shoot,

and I actually went,

but the school rules require you to fill out a travel form in advance to get permission to go out.

So I got called in by the school manager

and received a warning, haha.

Since 4 warnings means you can't stay at the school anymore,

I was thinking, "Well, one warning is no big deal!"

But then,

it turns out

if you go out without filling out the travel form and come back after midnight, you get 3 warnings at once.

Honestly, that was way more than I expected, haha.

Just once,

I got invited to a club by a model and had a good time,

and I get hit with 3 warnings for it... haha.

I mean, I'm not a kid anymore, and it was a school holiday, so going out should be fine! -- that's how I felt. But apparently those are the rules, so I accepted the warnings quietly, haha.

Well well, something interesting happened.

This kind of story is one of those experiences I can share precisely because this is a paid article, so I'll keep sharing things like this.

So for the remaining 3 weeks of school life,

I now have to be super well-behaved.

*I absolutely must follow curfew! haha

▼Tomorrow Is the Big Day!

Now then, tomorrow (April 9, 2024)

is the shoot with a hugely popular model in the Philippines.

I'm planning to do a photo shoot and short video shoot for the masu accessory "FOMUS Parure" as an official ambassador.

I'm still not 100% sure the model will actually show up,

so I can't say for certain,

but I'll give everything I've got.

A collaboration between a professional photographer

and a professional TikToker.

Well, let's see how it goes.

So, look forward to tomorrow's article!

title_en: Making It Happen No Matter What
excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Making It Happen No Matter What." ▼It was a winding road. Now, today was the Masu Photo shoot with a super popular pair of twin models in the Philippines.
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about

"Making It Happen No Matter What."

▼It Was a Winding Road

Now,

today was the Masu Photo shoot with

a super popular pair of twin models in the Philippines.

Getting this shoot set up was a winding road.

As I've written several times in these articles,

since arriving in Cebu in February, I've been using every school holiday to photograph local people willing to be subjects.

In the end, about 20 people participated,

which already exceeded my expectations.

I'm truly grateful to everyone who cooperated with FOMUS's Masu Photo shoots, and more than anything, I'm happy they became part of the FOMUS family.

But there's just one thing.

They don't have strong influence.

I don't mean that in a negative way at all.

When I think about "building the FOMUS community,"

my Instagram ad strategy is on the right track, I believe.

By doing free mutual photo shoots and talking about the FOMUS brand concept and community during and after the shoots, it creates a feeling of "maybe we can do something together!"

If we're going to expand FOMUS globally,

connections with local people

are incredibly important,

so this needs to be built up steadily over time.

However, when thinking about "generating revenue,"

the above ad strategy is a bit off.

Because

I, the representative who's supposed to be making money,

am paying Instagram for ads

and using my own time working without getting paid.

Honestly, when I run ads, thanks to the humor of the content, I get a ton of offers.

However, messages also come from people who don't even show their face, and spending time on that is wasteful.

That's why

I believed that for Masu Photo shoots,

professional models were necessary

for the overseas campaigns.

So, starting in February, alongside gathering local subjects, I was also

sending out tons of emails to professional models.

But the replies I got were just a handful.

No reply means:

- I simply couldn't convey the appeal or interest in writing

- The pay was too low

- The message didn't even reach them

- Their agency said no

Honestly, I thought to myself so many times, "Is the response really this low?"

So I changed my approach slightly.

I sent an email to just one group --

the ones I truly wanted to work with for Masu Photo.

And they replied.

What I changed in my approach was going from

"I'd like you to take a Masu Photo holding a masu" to

"I'd like you to be an ambassador for FOMUS Parure."

But I needed to be absolutely sure

this was someone who'd be suitable as an ambassador,

since it could cause problems down the road otherwise,

so this was the result of careful selection.

That one group was

the super popular twin models from the Philippines that I shot today.

*Left is Trisha, right is Aicy

Actually, these twins were

the ones I most wanted to photograph when I was researching Filipino models, by far.

The reasons were:

- They're cute (Masu Photo and accessories look great on them)

- They can leverage the unique advantage of being twins

- They're SNS pros, especially on TikTok

- They're models at a major production company

- They have great potential (they're 19)

And more.

For Masu Photo, they're perfect,

but for FOMUS Parure's target audience,

you might think they're too young.

However, FOMUS is built on the premise of growing the brand over the long term.

By having them connect with FOMUS

at this young age,

as they continue to grow

and FOMUS grows too,

working together 5 or 10 years from now will be so much easier.

Because they'll be

our connection in the Philippines.

Parure, the masu accessory line,

is a brand created to be a luxury content.

"Known by many, but not easily accessible"

-- that's what we're aiming for.

That's why, using the twins' current influence, we want to get FOMUS Parure

known by more people,

grow the brand,

and in a few years, work together on accessories or something else -- I don't know what exactly -- somewhere in the Philippines or the world.

In other words, it's a kind of investment.

For all these reasons, it had to be these twins!

I went all out with my approach, and

luckily, we got to do the shoot!

If it weren't for the masu accessory brand "Parure" that I launched, this shoot probably would never have happened.

What we can learn from this:

- "Masu Photo" is free and open to anyone in the world

- MaSU's "Masu Photo" is great for building community but runs at a loss

- "Masu Photo" with pros leads to brand awareness and profits through selling the Parure brand

In other words, there are two types of Masu Photo:

"For people who resonate with us, to grow the community"

and

"Serious model shoots for promotion."

Today was the latter.

That said, we end up talking during the shoot anyway, so it connects to building community too.

▼Working with Pros

Now, the backstory got a bit long,

but here's the shooting story.

The venue was

Movenpick Hotel Mactan Island Cebu (MOVENPICK HOTEL MACTAN ISLAND CEBU).

A 5-star hotel.

Why I chose this venue:

- Few local people around

- Wealthy guests staying there

- Day-use package includes beach, pool, and lunch

They have 7.6 million TikTok followers, making them incredibly famous,

and if I'd brought them to a place like Ayala Mall where we shot in February, people other than me would have swarmed around and we wouldn't have been able to shoot at all, haha.

My read on this was actually correct --

the hotel was practically empty in the morning.

So from the moment we gathered until the shoot,

we could proceed without anyone getting in the way.

However,

the hotel staff noticed right away,

and even the hotel manager came over to greet them, haha.

*The hotel side must have been happy too

So,

the hotel staff kept an eye on things during the shoot.

It was scorching hot today,

so we mostly shot indoors,

and only went outside for the pool and beach.

Still, the shoot took about 90 minutes.

*I wanted to shoot quickly and wrap it up

*For both our sakes

During the shoot,

we had lots of great conversations.

They're hugely popular TikTokers,

and any Filipino would be envious of the situation,

but for me,

I was desperately focused on "FOMUS promotion & English practice," haha.

That said, studying in Cebu is really paying off --

my English conversations have improved dramatically compared to 2 months ago.

The conversations just keep flowing!!

This time, the main focus wasn't Masu Photo

but "FOMUS Parure," which was a fresh change.

I had them change accessories about 3 times.

After the shoot, the three of us went for hotel lunch.

We came on a day-use pass again,

and it was 3,000 pesos per person, 9,000 pesos total, which I covered. *About 24,000 yen

Of that, 7,500 pesos could be used for dining.

*About 20,000 yen

We headed to lunch just past 11:30.

*Before it got crowded

Hotel lunch is pricey on the menu,

but in my mind, just being able to share a meal with the twins made it worth every peso.

*Post-work communication is important

We had great conversations here too,

and hearing their stories was really interesting.

After lunch, the twins headed home.

They immediately posted the FOMUS shoot on their Instagram Stories!

*That really made me happy

▼After the Shoot

I stayed at the hotel and started editing the photos from the shoot.

Something funny happened --

I was editing in the lounge,

and a hotel staff member kept coming over

asking, "Would you like a drink?"

Actually, I still had 2,200 pesos left on the 7,500-peso meal credit, so she was encouraging me to use it.

So I ordered a pineapple juice.

I got to work on my editing in an incredibly luxurious setting.

Then, since I still had points left, I even had dinner before leaving.

To use up the remaining points,

I ordered their signature mojito, a hamburger,

and even a cheesecake, haha.

Even so, I only used 6,600 pesos,

and couldn't use up the remaining 900 pesos, haha.

I never expected it to turn out like this!!

▼Making It Happen No Matter What

Now,

someone like me, with zero connections in the Philippines,

has to use every skill and piece of content I have,

combined with overwhelming initiative,

and forcefully pull things my way

through any method with even a sliver of possibility.

I came to Cebu in February, and while attending language school,

I was endlessly sending out emails.

To FOMUS members and close friends,

I kept saying out loud,

"I want to shoot with professional models for FOMUS."

*I think I've written about it in this activity log too

And after trying various approaches,

the twin models I most wanted to photograph

took interest in the masu accessory brand

"FOMUS Parure" that I launched last year,

and they'll even serve as official ambassadors!

There are many things that I and FOMUS currently lack,

but "influence" in particular isn't something you can build overnight,

so I felt the support of people who already have that power was necessary. Being able to connect at this timing was lucky!

Through this, the path to expanding FOMUS brand awareness

and how to present it to the world

has become clearer than before,

and more than anything, I hope the many people who support me daily

can see that FOMUS and my activities

are connecting to the world.

There's something I've decided for myself:

"I'll make the path I chose the right one."

Currently, FOMUS is the only brand that turns masu into entertainment and uses it as a communication tool.

There's absolutely nothing like a guaranteed formula for success.

That's exactly why I keep a list of "things that will definitely lead to failure."

Coming to Cebu for language study,

starting from zero connections,

within 2 months I connected with

the incredibly popular Filipino model twins with 7.6 million TikTok followers.

I don't think numbers are everything,

but these aren't people you can casually call up.

On top of that, they've given permission to use their photos on our accessory brand's website.

Since coming to Cebu, I can feel that the scale of my activities

and FOMUS's work is gradually growing,

and that excites me.

What's especially significant is that

the embarrassment of speaking English (the kind that comes from not being perfect) has completely vanished.

In other words, working with people overseas who don't speak Japanese has become incredibly fun.

I'm certainly not

fluent in English,

and I can't understand everything I hear,

but I do have a sense of ease with English now.

All of this combined,

I'm developing the ability to

forcefully turn any option into the right answer

in order to spread FOMUS.

This ability is essential for a business owner,

so I'll keep honing it.

Lastly,

I'm so glad I got to take Masu Photo pictures

with the twins I most wanted to shoot!!

title_en: Sharing Where We Are and Where We're Going
excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Since the crowdfunding launches tomorrow, I'll be talking about "Sharing Where We Are and Where We're Going." This article is also specially written on note, so you can read it there too.
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Since the crowdfunding launches tomorrow,

I'll be talking about

"Sharing Where We Are and Where We're Going."

This article is also specially written on note,

so you can read it there too.

Sharing Where We Are and Where We're Going | MaSU [FOMUS]

▼Sharing Where We Are and Where We're Going

There's just 1 day left until the crowdfunding begins.

#Starting at 6 PM on the 11th

Today, I'll share the "destination" I'm aiming for

through the masu brand "FOMUS" and our current "location."

#It's a bit long but I'd be happy if you read it

#I'll keep it as concise as possible

[About FOMUS]

FOMUS's main content is

the traditional Japanese craft "masu."

About 1,300 years ago, it was used as a measuring instrument,

but today, it's used for drinking sake, as commemorative gifts,

and for various other purposes.

At FOMUS, we respect those "functions" while

adding the element of "meaning" as a "community."

For example, the masu we handle at FOMUS

has the "FOMUS logo" on it.

Since November 2021, I've been an address hopper traveling all over Japan,

taking photos with local models

while holding masu (Masu Photo),

collaborating with people who have specialized skills (art),

and hosting events.

#I also attended as many interesting drinking gatherings as possible

As a result, the shops and people I encountered in the places I traveled to now own FOMUS masu.

And now, 3 years since launching FOMUS,

people who own masu are visiting shops and meeting other masu owners

in different locations.

In other words, the FOMUS masu has become

a "communication tool" that connects people.

In today's world,

opportunities for adults to connect in real life are decreasing.

But our content is "masu,"

and we can do a universal "cheers" with it!

#You can cheers even without alcohol

▼Destination (The World We're Aiming For)

Over these 3 years,

the FOMUS masu has become a tool that "connects people."

At events in Tokyo, people came from as far as Aomori, Osaka,

and even Taiwan -- a diverse range of people participated

and enjoyed connecting.

And everyone has some kind of strength or talent.

At FOMUS, people are free

to leverage those strengths.

For example, last year we launched "FOMUS Parure,"

a brand specializing in masu accessories,

which has been extremely popular with people overseas.

Actually, these accessories weren't my idea --

a model who had been doing Masu Photo shoots since the early days

originally made a custom one,

and that became the catalyst to "turn it into a brand."

Like this, we've become able to create new value

together with the people involved in FOMUS.

#We can now generate profits too

And with the pandemic over,

the global expansion we'd been planning

since the brand's launch has become possible!

When creating FOMUS services and content,

I've made it a rule to only use

"non-verbal tools" or "things with a low translation barrier."

This is crucial for global expansion.

#The word for "cheers" differs by country but everyone knows it

#Photography is universal too

Through FOMUS, we'll connect the world

and create an environment where people can engage

regardless of borders, gender, or religion!

By the way, FOMUS's name is

a portmanteau of camera "FOCUS" and "masu."

#You, holding a FOMUS masu, are the star

I will focus on you with Masu!

FOMUS is where anyone can become the star using masu.

And this year, we'll be expanding the FOMUS brand

primarily in Europe, connecting people

and creating unprecedented value and meaning.

▼Current Location (Current Challenges)

So, where do things stand now?

It's been 3 years since launching FOMUS.

After moving non-stop, here's what I've learned:

- Japanese people in their 20s and older know about "masu" but don't use them

- Some teenagers don't even know what they are

- In Japan, "masu" is strongly associated with sake

- People who own FOMUS masu are starting to connect with each other

- Overseas, showing people masu fascinates them -- it's a "traditional craft"

- Overseas, both masu and masu accessories are popular

- Overseas, since masu isn't associated with sake,

it's easier to convey FOMUS's concept

European people visiting Japan

seem especially interested in masu.

The reason? It's easy to bring home as a "souvenir"!

#There are other reasons too

In 2023, I was active in Japan, Taiwan, and Malaysia,

and currently I'm doing FOMUS activities in the Philippines.

Last year, my English skills were lacking,

and while I could do Masu Photo shoots,

communication was a challenge.

As the representative of FOMUS,

I realized, "I can't expand overseas without English!"

So since February this year, on weekdays I study at a language school in Cebu,

and on weekends, I do Masu Photo shoots

to find people who resonate with FOMUS!

I'm still in the middle of language school,

but my English conversation ability has improved

dramatically compared to last year in Malaysia.

Thanks to that, connections keep growing.

#Communication is so incredibly important

I'm glad I realized this

while still in my 20s.

I went off on a slight tangent,

but the point is, English skills are necessary,

and I'm in the process of overcoming that,

while building stronger connections with local people.

Furthermore, while I had zero connections in the Philippines initially, by using my "make-it-happen power,"

I was able to work with professional models.

#This was entirely thanks to the masu accessories

Right now, I'm using every weapon that I and FOMUS have

to forcefully pull things in a positive direction.

That's what it takes --

someone like me can't create value any other way.

As FOMUS's representative,

my role is to bring in

"People, Products, Money, and Information."

I've invested a lot of money and time

into spreading FOMUS,

and thanks to that, I've gained connections and environments.

What I can do now in my 20s is earn money

while throwing away unnecessary pride and "raising money."

Earning money is important,

but for big endeavors, you need seed capital.

The Europe expansion requires at least 10 million yen,

and FOMUS's current budget can't cover that.

So this time, I'll be using crowdfunding

to help fund part of our activities.

FOMUS

is going to expand further around the world, deepening connections.

No brand or person has ever specialized in the traditional craft "masu"

and built a community that connects people, until now.

It was uncharted territory even for me,

but after running hard for 3 years, I'm now

convinced that FOMUS can "make the lives of everyone involved more enjoyable."

To connect people around the world

and create a world where we can cheers with masu,

I'd love your support.

And in the near future, when FOMUS becomes even more exciting,

let's all have a big cheers together!!

Crowdfunding starts tomorrow at 6 PM!

The first week is super important,

so please, your support means everything!!

excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Crowdfunding Has Launched." ▼The crowdfunding has begun! Now, the crowdfunding to help fund part of the Europe expedition starting this July has launched.
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about

"Crowdfunding Has Launched."

▼The Crowdfunding Has Begun!

Now, the crowdfunding to help fund part of the Europe expedition starting this July has launched.

Here's the crowdfunding page:

https://camp-fire.jp/projects/view/733462?utm_campaign=cp_po_share_c_msg_mypage_projects_show

It will run from April 11 to June 16.

That said, the initial crowdfunding buzz will probably

come not today but on the 13th and 14th.

The reason being it's a weekend,

and it's also my birthday.

This is just speculation,

and I think the results will reflect what I've built up through my activities so far.

While I'm in the Philippines, I'll focus on spreading awareness that the crowdfunding is happening,

and after I return to Japan, I'll do grassroots outreach.

Now, anyone reading this article later on will already know the results.

That's kind of interesting too, haha.

See you next time!

Help Spread the Traditional Craft "Masu" Brand "FOMUS" to the World!!
